title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The oven main control board and display board cannot communicate properly.
fullDescription: Error E01 on Thermador ovens indicates a communication failure between the main control board (relay board) and the user interface display board. These boards exchange data over a serial link. If interrupted by faulty wiring, a loose connector, or a failed board, the oven cannot receive user commands or display status.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable or wiring harness between boards is loose or damaged
User interface display board has failed
Main control board communication circuit has failed
Power surge damaged communication circuits

symptoms: E01 displayed on the oven screen
Oven does not respond to button presses
Display flickers or goes blank intermittently
Oven shuts off unexpectedly during cooking
Clock and timer functions do not work

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the oven at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Remove the back panel to access the control boards. Check all ribbon cables and wire harness connectors for secure seating.

fixSteps.title: Reseat all connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and firmly reconnect all ribbon cables and wire harnesses between the main control board and display board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating connectors does not resolve E01, the display board or main control board may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connectors does not fix the error, control board replacement should be handled by an authorized Thermador technician.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E01 control board communication error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E01?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the communication circuits on either control board. Use a dedicated surge protector on the oven circuit.

faq.q: Is it the display board or the main board?
faq.a: If the display is completely blank, the display board is more likely. If the display works but does not respond to inputs, the main board or wiring is more suspect.

faq.q: Can I replace a Thermador oven control board myself?
faq.a: Technically yes, but it involves high-voltage wiring. Thermador recommends authorized service for board replacement.

title: Oven Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor (RTD) has an open circuit, preventing temperature monitoring.
fullDescription: Error E02 indicates the Thermador oven RTD (Resistance Temperature Detector) sensor has developed an open circuit. The control board cannot read the oven cavity temperature, making it impossible to regulate heating. The sensor, its wiring, or the connector may be at fault.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor probe has failed with an open circuit
Wiring to the sensor is broken or has a loose connection
Connector at the control board is corroded or disconnected
Control board sensor input circuit has failed

symptoms: E02 error on the display
Oven does not heat at all
Oven heats without stopping (runaway heating)
Temperature display shows incorrect readings
Error appears immediately when a bake cycle is started

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and try a bake cycle. If E02 returns, the sensor needs testing.

fixSteps.title: Locate and test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The RTD sensor probe is at the upper rear wall of the oven cavity. Disconnect the sensor wire at the back. Measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 1080-1100 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring continuity
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests good, check wiring from the sensor connector to the control board for continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor reads open circuit (OL), remove the mounting screw, pull the probe out, route the new sensor wire, and reinstall.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test good, the control board input circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E02 temperature sensor replacement

faq.q: What is an RTD sensor?
faq.a: RTD stands for Resistance Temperature Detector. It measures temperature by changing electrical resistance as the oven heats. Thermador ovens use platinum RTD sensors.

faq.q: Can I use a generic temperature sensor?
faq.a: It must be the correct resistance type for your Thermador model. Using the wrong sensor will cause inaccurate temperature readings.

faq.q: Is E02 dangerous?
faq.a: Potentially. Without temperature monitoring, the oven could overheat. Do not use the oven until E02 is resolved.

title: Oven Temperature Sensor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor has a short circuit, providing zero or near-zero resistance.
fullDescription: Error E03 indicates the oven RTD temperature sensor has developed a short circuit, reading near zero ohms instead of the expected 1080 ohms at room temperature. This gives the control board a falsely high temperature reading and prevents normal operation.

causes.cause: Temperature sensor insulation has broken down from heat exposure
Sensor wiring is shorted due to heat damage or pinching
Moisture has entered the sensor or connector
Control board sensor circuit has a fault

symptoms: E03 error on the display
Oven will not heat
Display may show extremely high false temperature
Error appears as soon as bake is selected
Oven may have overheated before the error appeared

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Disconnect the sensor at the back of the oven. Measure resistance with a multimeter. Near zero ohms confirms a short circuit.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the sensor wire
fixSteps.instruction: Check the sensor wire routing inside the oven cavity for melted insulation or wires touching the oven walls.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw from the oven wall, pull the old sensor out, feed the new sensor wire through, and secure.

fixSteps.title: Test after replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Restore power and run a bake cycle. Verify the temperature reading climbs normally.

whenToCallPro: If a new sensor still shows E03, the control board or wiring harness needs professional attention.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E03 temperature sensor short circuit fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E02 and E03?
faq.a: E02 indicates an open circuit (infinite resistance), while E03 indicates a short circuit (near zero resistance). Both require sensor replacement.

faq.q: Can heat damage the temperature sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Extended self-clean cycles at very high temperatures can degrade the sensor insulation over time.

faq.q: Should I avoid self-clean to protect the sensor?
faq.a: Self-clean is designed to be safe, but limiting frequency to a few times per year can extend sensor life.

title: Oven Overtemperature Protection
shortDescription: The oven has exceeded the maximum safe temperature, triggering the overtemperature cutoff.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates the Thermador oven safety thermostat has detected the oven cavity temperature exceeded the maximum safe limit. This safety system shuts down all heating elements to prevent damage or fire hazard. The oven must cool down completely before reset.

causes.cause: Bake or broil element relay on the control board is stuck closed
Temperature sensor is inaccurate causing overheating before detection
Door seal is damaged allowing the oven to work harder
Cooling fan failure causing excess heat buildup

symptoms: E06 error on the display
Oven is extremely hot, much hotter than set temperature
Oven has shut itself off during cooking
Burning smell from the oven
Door is too hot to touch

fixSteps.title: Turn off the oven and let it cool
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker immediately. Open the oven door slightly and allow the oven to cool completely, 1-2 hours.

fixSteps.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Once cool, test the RTD sensor. At room temperature it should read about 1080 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the oven door gasket for tears, gaps, or compression damage.

fixSteps.title: Check the cooling fan
fixSteps.instruction: The cooling fan should run whenever the oven is hot. Listen for it during operation.

whenToCallPro: If E06 recurs after sensor replacement, the control board relay may be stuck and requires professional diagnosis. This is a safety issue.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E06 overtemperature safety fix

faq.q: Is E06 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. An oven exceeding its safe temperature limit poses a fire risk. Turn off the breaker immediately and have it repaired before use.

faq.q: Can I reset E06?
faq.a: After the oven cools completely, turn off the breaker for 10 minutes then restore power. If it recurs, stop using the oven.

faq.q: Could a stuck relay cause E06?
faq.a: Yes. If the heating element relay is stuck closed, the element will continue heating regardless of the set temperature.

title: Door Lock Mechanism Failure
shortDescription: The oven door lock motor or switch is not functioning correctly, affecting self-clean operation.
fullDescription: Error E08 indicates the Thermador oven door lock mechanism has failed. The door lock is essential for self-clean cycles where temperatures reach 900F. The lock motor may be unable to engage or disengage, or the switch may not confirm the locked state.

causes.cause: Door lock motor has failed
Door lock switch is not registering the locked position
Lock mechanism is physically jammed or misaligned
Wiring to the lock assembly is damaged

symptoms: E08 error on the display
Self-clean cycle will not start
Door lock motor makes grinding noise
Door is locked and will not unlock after cooling
Self-clean cycle stops prematurely

fixSteps.title: Power reset the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes to reset the lock motor to default position.

fixSteps.title: Manually release the lock if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: Access the manual lock release through the top vent or by removing the top panel. Move the lock latch to unlocked.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the lock motor behind the front panel. Test for voltage during a self-clean attempt and check motor continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the front panel screws, disconnect the old assembly, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the door is stuck locked and you cannot release it manually, or if the lock assembly replacement does not resolve E08.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E08 door lock motor replacement

faq.q: My oven door is stuck locked with E08. What do I do?
faq.a: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes. If it remains locked, use the manual release from the top panel. Never force the door.

faq.q: Can I still bake with E08?
faq.a: Yes, normal bake and broil do not require the door lock. Only self-clean is affected.

faq.q: Why does the door lock for self-clean?
faq.a: Self-clean reaches approximately 900F. The lock is a safety feature to prevent accidental opening.

title: Bake Element Failure
shortDescription: The bake (bottom) heating element has failed or its circuit is broken.
fullDescription: Error E11 indicates the Thermador oven bake element at the bottom of the cavity is not drawing current when activated. The element may have burned through, a wire connection may be loose, or the control board relay may not be sending power.

causes.cause: Bake element has burned out or developed a break
Wire connection to the bake element is loose or burned
Control board bake relay has failed
Safety thermostat has tripped

symptoms: E11 error on the display
Oven does not heat on bake mode
Broil mode may still work
Visible burn spot or break in the bake element
Food on the bottom rack does not cook

fixSteps.title: Visually inspect the bake element
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the bake element at the bottom of the oven for burn marks, blistering, holes, or breaks.

fixSteps.title: Test the bake element
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Disconnect the element wires. Measure resistance across terminals. A good element reads 20-50 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check wire connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wire terminals for burned or melted connectors. Replace damaged connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the bake element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screws, pull the element forward, disconnect wires, connect the new element, and secure.

whenToCallPro: If the element and wiring test good, the control board bake relay may need professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E11 bake element replacement

faq.q: Can I still use broil if I have E11?
faq.a: Often yes, since the broil element is separate. However, resolve E11 before continued use.

faq.q: How long do Thermador bake elements last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years depending on usage. Frequent self-clean cycles can shorten element life.

faq.q: Is it safe to replace a bake element myself?
faq.a: Yes, as long as you turn off the circuit breaker first. The element is accessible from inside the oven.

title: Broil Element Failure
shortDescription: The broil (top) heating element has failed or its circuit is open.
fullDescription: Error E12 indicates the Thermador oven broil element at the top of the cavity is not drawing current when activated. The element may have burned out, a wire connection may be faulty, or the relay may have failed.

causes.cause: Broil element has burned out
Wire connection to the broil element is loose or burned
Control board broil relay has failed
High-limit thermostat has tripped

symptoms: E12 error on the display
No heat from the top element when broiling
Bake mode may still work normally
Food on the top rack does not brown
Convection bake has uneven heat

fixSteps.title: Visually inspect the broil element
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the broil element at the top of the oven cavity for damage, holes, or breaks.

fixSteps.title: Test the broil element
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Disconnect wires. Measure resistance. A good element reads 20-50 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check wire connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wire terminals for burned or melted connectors. Repair or replace.

fixSteps.title: Replace the broil element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screws, pull forward slightly, disconnect wires, connect new element, and secure.

whenToCallPro: If the element and wiring test good but E12 persists, the control board broil relay may need professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E12 broil element replacement

faq.q: Can I bake without the broil element?
faq.a: Basic baking uses the bottom element. However, convection and browning functions rely on the broil element.

faq.q: Why did my broil element burn out?
faq.a: Broil elements operate at very high temperatures and eventually fail from thermal stress. Self-clean accelerates wear.

faq.q: Are Thermador oven elements interchangeable with Bosch?
faq.a: Some models share elements as both are BSH brands. Verify the part number matches your specific model.

title: Meat Probe Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The internal meat probe sensor is reading incorrectly or has an open/short circuit.
fullDescription: Error E13 indicates the Thermador oven meat probe sensor is providing out-of-range readings. The probe measures food internal temperature and signals the oven to stop when the target is reached. A faulty probe, damaged cable, or corroded jack can trigger this error.

causes.cause: Meat probe sensor is damaged or has an open circuit
Probe jack inside the oven is corroded or dirty
Probe cable is damaged from heat exposure
Control board probe input circuit is faulty

symptoms: E13 error when the meat probe is inserted
Temperature reading from the probe is erratic
Oven keeps cooking past the probe target
Probe reading shows max or min temperature immediately
Error only appears when probe feature is selected

fixSteps.title: Inspect and clean the probe and jack
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the meat probe. Clean the probe tip and plug contacts. Clean the jack inside the oven with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Test the meat probe
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the probe plug pins. At room temperature a typical probe reads around 50k ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the meat probe
fixSteps.instruction: If the probe tests faulty, plug the new probe into the jack and test.

fixSteps.title: Test the probe jack
fixSteps.instruction: If a new probe still triggers E13, check wiring continuity from the jack to the control board.

whenToCallPro: If a new probe does not fix E13, the probe jack or control board input may need professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E13 meat probe replacement

faq.q: Can I use the oven without the meat probe?
faq.a: Yes. Simply do not insert the probe or select the probe function. The oven operates normally without it.

faq.q: Can I use a third-party meat probe?
faq.a: Only use probes designed for your Thermador model. Third-party probes may have different resistance values.

faq.q: Why does my meat probe read wrong temperatures?
faq.a: Heat exposure can degrade the probe sensor accuracy over time. Replace if readings are consistently off by more than 10 degrees.

title: Convection Fan Motor Failure
shortDescription: The convection fan motor is not operating, preventing even heat distribution.
fullDescription: Error E15 on Thermador ovens indicates the convection fan motor is not running when activated by the control board. The convection fan circulates hot air for even cooking. When it fails, convection bake and roast functions will not work properly.

causes.cause: Convection fan motor has burned out
Fan blade is obstructed or jammed
Wiring to the fan motor is damaged
Control board fan relay has failed

symptoms: E15 error on the display
No air circulation sound during convection modes
Uneven cooking and browning
Convection bake takes much longer
Hot spots in the oven cavity

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the convection fan at the back wall. Ensure nothing blocks the blade.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Access the motor from the back. Disconnect wiring and test resistance.

fixSteps.title: Spin the fan manually
fixSteps.instruction: Try spinning the fan blade by hand. It should rotate freely. If stiff, bearings may have seized.

fixSteps.title: Replace the convection fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the back panel inside the oven, remove the fan blade and motor screws, disconnect wiring, install new motor.

whenToCallPro: If the motor tests good but does not run, the control board relay or wiring may need professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E15 convection fan motor replacement

faq.q: Can I bake without convection if E15 is active?
faq.a: Standard bake uses only the bottom element without the fan. You can still use conventional bake.

faq.q: Why is my convection fan noisy?
faq.a: A noisy fan indicates worn bearings. Replace the motor proactively before it fails completely.

faq.q: Does the convection fan run during self-clean?
faq.a: No. The convection fan is not used during self-clean to protect the motor from extreme temperatures.

title: Cooling Fan Failure
shortDescription: The oven cooling fan is not operating, risking overheating of control components.
fullDescription: Error E18 on Thermador ovens indicates the cooling fan that protects control electronics and surrounding cabinetry is not running. This fan activates when the oven is hot and continues after shutdown until cool.

causes.cause: Cooling fan motor has failed
Fan blade is blocked or broken
Wiring to the cooling fan is damaged
Control board fan output circuit failure

symptoms: E18 error on the display
Control panel area is very hot
No fan noise from the top vent during or after cooking
Surrounding cabinets feel hot
Oven may shut down to protect electronics

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the oven top vent is not blocked by items placed on top. Clear any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Listen for the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Turn on the oven to 350F. Within a few minutes the cooling fan should start.

fixSteps.title: Test the cooling fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker. Access the fan from the back or top. Test for continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the cooling fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old motor, disconnect wiring, mount the new motor, reconnect, and test.

whenToCallPro: If the cooling fan motor tests good but does not run, the control board or wiring needs professional attention.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E18 cooling fan motor replacement

faq.q: Is it safe to use the oven with E18?
faq.a: No. Without the cooling fan, control electronics and cabinetry can overheat, potentially causing damage or fire. Do not use until repaired.

faq.q: Why does my oven fan keep running after I turn it off?
faq.a: This is normal. The cooling fan continues until the oven cools to a safe temperature.

faq.q: Can a blocked vent cause E18?
faq.a: Not directly, but a blocked vent can cause the fan to work harder and fail prematurely.

title: Control Board EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The control board internal memory (EEPROM) has a read/write error.
fullDescription: Error E20 indicates the main control board EEPROM has encountered a failure. The EEPROM stores calibration data, cycle settings, and configuration parameters. When it fails, the oven may lose calibration or fail to start cycles.

causes.cause: EEPROM chip on the control board has degraded
Power interruption corrupted EEPROM data
Electrical spike damaged the memory circuit
Control board manufacturing defect

symptoms: E20 error on the display
Oven loses temperature calibration repeatedly
Clock resets after every power cycle
Cycle settings are not saved
Random or inconsistent oven behavior

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes. Restore power. A reset may clear temporary data corruption.

fixSteps.title: Re-enter calibration settings
fixSteps.instruction: If the oven starts after reset, re-enter calibration offsets and user settings.

fixSteps.title: Monitor for recurrence
fixSteps.instruction: Use the oven normally for a few days. If E20 returns, the EEPROM is failing.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If E20 persists, the main control board must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: E20 almost always requires professional service for control board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E20 EEPROM control board replacement

faq.q: Can E20 fix itself?
faq.a: Sometimes a power reset clears temporary data corruption. If E20 recurs, the EEPROM is failing and the board needs replacement.

faq.q: Does the new board need programming?
faq.a: Some replacement boards come pre-programmed. Others may require a technician to load firmware.

faq.q: Will I lose calibration with a new board?
faq.a: Yes. A new board has default settings. You will need to recalibrate the oven temperature offset.
